text: Jacob went on his way and came to the land of the people of the east.
He saw a well in a field with three flocks of sheep lying by it, covered by a great stone.
All the flocks would gather there, and the shepherds would roll the stone away, water the sheep, then put it back.
Jacob asked the shepherds where they were from; they said Haran.
He asked if they knew Laban, son of Nahor; they said yes.
He asked if Laban was well; they said yes, and that his daughter Rachel was coming with the sheep.
Jacob suggested it was still early and they should water the flocks and go back to pasture.
They said they had to wait until all the flocks gathered and the stone was rolled away together.
While he was speaking, Rachel arrived with her father's sheep, since she was a shepherdess.
When Jacob saw Rachel and Laban's sheep, he rolled the stone away and watered the flock himself.
Jacob kissed Rachel and wept aloud.
He told her he was Rebekah's son, her father's relative; she ran to tell her father.
Laban heard, ran to meet Jacob, embraced him, and brought him home; Jacob told him everything.
Laban said, surely you are my own flesh and bone, and Jacob stayed with him a month.
Laban asked Jacob what wages he wanted for his work, since he shouldn't serve for nothing.
Laban had two daughters: Leah, the older, and Rachel, the younger.
Leah's eyes were weak, but Rachel was beautiful in form and appearance.
Jacob loved Rachel and offered to serve seven years for her.
Laban agreed it was better to give her to Jacob than to another man.
Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and they seemed like only a few days because of his love for her.
Jacob told Laban his time was up and asked for his wife.
Laban gathered the men of the place and made a feast.
That evening he brought Leah instead, and Jacob slept with her.
Laban gave his servant Zilpah to Leah as her maid.
In the morning Jacob saw it was Leah and confronted Laban about the deception.
Laban said it wasn't their custom to give the younger before the firstborn.
He told Jacob to finish Leah's bridal week, then he could have Rachel too for another seven years' service.
Jacob agreed, finished the week, and Laban gave him Rachel as wife also.
Laban gave his servant Bilhah to Rachel as her maid.
Jacob slept with Rachel too, and loved her more than Leah, serving another seven years.
Seeing Leah was unloved, the LORD opened her womb, while Rachel remained childless.
Leah conceived and bore a son, naming him Reuben, saying the LORD had seen her affliction.
She conceived again and bore a son named Simeon, saying the LORD heard she was unloved.
She bore a third son named Levi, hoping now her husband would be attached to her.
She bore a fourth son named Judah, saying now she would praise the LORD; then she stopped bearing for a time.
